response:
Let’s check if \( 22733 \) is prime.  

First, test small primes:  
- Not divisible by 2 (odd).  
- Sum of digits \( 2+2+7+3+3 = 17 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Doesn’t end in 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.  
- \( 22733 \div 7 = 3247.57\ldots \) (not integer).  

We can check divisibility by primes up to \( \sqrt{22733} \approx 150.77 \).  

Test 11: alternating sum of digits \( (2-2+7-3+3) = 7 \), not divisible by 11.  

Test 13: \( 13 \times 1748 = 22724 \), remainder 9 → no.  

Test 17: \( 17 \times 1337 = 22729 \), remainder 4 → no.  

Test 19: \( 19 \times 1196 = 22724 \), remainder 9 → no.  

Test 23: \( 23 \times 988 = 22724 \), remainder 9 → no.  

Test 29: \( 29 \times 784 = 22736 \), too high; \( 29 \times 783 = 22707 \), remainder 26 → no.  

Test 31: \( 31 \times 733 = 22723 \), remainder 10 → no.  

Test 37: \( 37 \times 614 = 22718 \), remainder 15 → no.  

Test 41: \( 41 \times 554 = 22714 \), remainder 19 → no.  

Test 43: \( 43 \times 528 = 22704 \), remainder 29 → no.  

Test 47: \( 47 \times 483 = 22701 \), remainder 32 → no.  

Test 53: \( 53 \times 429 = 22737 \), too high; \( 53 \times 428 = 22684 \), remainder 49 → no.  

Test 59: \( 59 \times 385 = 22715 \), remainder 18 → no.  

Test 61: \( 61 \times 372 = 22692 \), remainder 41 → no.  

Test 67: \( 67 \times 339 = 22713 \), remainder 20 → no.  

Test 71: \( 71 \times 320 = 22720 \), remainder 13 → no.  

Test 73: \( 73 \times 311 = 22703 \), remainder 30 → no.  

Test 79: \( 79 \times 287 = 22673 \), remainder 60 → no.  

Test 83: \( 83 \times 273 = 22659 \), remainder 74 → no.  

Test 89: \( 89 \times 255 = 22695 \), remainder 38 → no.  

Test 97: \( 97 \times 234 = 22698 \), remainder 35 → no.  

Test 101: \( 101 \times 225 = 22725 \), remainder 8 → no.  

Test 103: \( 103 \times 220 = 22660 \), remainder 73 → no.  

Test 107: \( 107 \times 212 = 22684 \), remainder 49 → no.  

Test 109: \( 109 \times 208 = 22672 \), remainder 61 → no.  

Test 113: \( 113 \times 201 = 22713 \), remainder 20 → no.  

Test 127: \( 127 \times 179 = 22733 \) exactly.  

So \( 22733 = 127 \times 179 \), both primes.  

Thus \( 22733 \) is **not prime**.  

**Answer:** no
